required_preferences.fact: Prepares only low-cholesterol, non-fried, unprocessed dinners for her father in advance, aligning meal prep for both their health needs

supersedes.fact: Prepares simple, reheatable dinners for her father in advance three times a week, adjusting meal options for his post-stroke dietary needs

required_preferences.fact: Leaves all work devices at the office on in-office days and avoids work emails after her commute home — does not perform client work from her bedroom on required office days.

supersedes.fact: Keeps her work devices and email open until 10pm during intensive audit weeks — sometimes works from her bedroom for late-night client revisions

required_preferences.fact: Chooses to carpool with a colleague on in-office days to avoid parking stress and use the commuting time for decompressing or peer support.
Schedules all daughter-related appointments and activities at least one week ahead to avoid last-minute conflicts while solo parenting
Begins CPAP machine setup and bedroom wind-down routine at exactly the same time each night, allowing no more than 20 minutes of variation, to maintain sleep schedule consistency.
Blocks Tuesday and Thursday mornings for uninterrupted strategy planning — never schedules client-focused work or team trainings during these blocks.

persona: A mid-career accountant at a regional firm specializing in nonprofit organizations
